commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the patch has no discrete blocking finding and should proceed through normal maintainer review, CI, and optional live smoke proof.; Cleared: The diff stays within WhatsApp plugin code, tests, and changelog, and preserves existing media local-root/read-capability plumbing without new dependencies, workflows, secrets, or package-resolution changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds WhatsApp plugin support for shared `message(action="upload-file")` path/URL media by advertising the action, routing it through `sendMessageWhatsApp`, rejecting buffer-only payloads, and adding tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main dispatches WhatsApp `upload-file` to the plugin action path, but the WhatsApp plugin only supports `react`, so dispatch returns unsupported without needing a live WhatsApp account.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the patch has no discrete blocking finding and should proceed through normal maintainer review, CI, and optional live smoke proof.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): lower upload-file media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Lower WhatsApp-targeted `message(action="upload-file")` calls with path/URL media into the existing `send` + `media` path.
- Keep WhatsApp's native action discovery unchanged; this only handles shared-message-tool calls that resolve to WhatsApp at execution time.
- Reject buffer-only WhatsApp upload-file payloads loudly because they cannot be safely represented by the send-media transport.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Behavior addressed: A shared `message` tool call using `action=upload-file` with a media path/URL no longer fails after resolving to WhatsApp; it is delivered through WhatsApp's supported send-media path.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with targeted Vitest coverage and changed-file validation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`; `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests pass for WhatsApp upload-file path lowering and buffer rejection, while existing WhatsApp action discovery tests remain unchanged.

Observed result after fix: Targeted tests report 2 passed files / 35 passed tests, Plugin SDK API baseline check reports OK, and `pnpm check:changed` completes successfully.

What was not tested: A live WhatsApp Web send was not run from this branch; coverage uses the outbound runner and WhatsApp action discovery tests.
target.author: ngutman
